title: brassica oleracea var. alboglabra
intro: Brassica oleracea var. alboglabra, also known as Chinese broccoli or gai lan, is a variety of the Brassica oleracea plant family, which includes cabbage, cauliflower, and kale. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of gai lan include reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain cancers, as well as supporting digestive health and immune function. Gai lan can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, capsules, and infusions, and is also commonly consumed as a steamed or stir-fried vegetable in Asian cuisine.
